title: Cyathea subdubia
uses: Cyathea subdubia is reportedly used in traditional medicine, with its leaves and roots said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Cyathea subdubia for medicinal use.
taxonomy: Cyathea subdubia is classified as a species of fern in the family Cyatheaceae. This plant is a type of tree fern native to New Zealand. The classification of Cyathea subdubia is further divided into the genus Cyathea and the specific epithet subdubia, indicating its unique characteristics within the genus. The classification of Cyathea subdubia reflects its distinct evolutionary history and relationships with other tree ferns.
